rationale.R1.2: The paragraph makes two logically questionable moves: (1) asserting as settled fact that deep neural detectors 'uniformly outperform' classical baselines 'across the full range of anomaly-detection settings' without citation, and (2) claiming any downstream AMT quality lift can be 'attributed entirely to the gating step' while ignoring confounds — both are load-bearing argument flaws a review would surface.
rationale.R1.3: The dominance claim for Deep SVDD over isolation forest, LOF, and one-class SVM is asserted as already established but no reference is given; the causal attribution of quality lift to the gate rests on an unverified exclusion of alternative explanations. Evidence-to-claim alignment is materially at issue.
rationale.R1.6: The second paragraph is a sustained run-on: a single sentence chains 'first encoded ... the encoder is ... and a Deep SVDD head was then trained ... the head trains ... and converges ... and at inference time we will score ... and candidates above ...' across roughly seven clauses. Prose quality is independently a plausible primary concern given the open-ended delegation.
